title: '80s sci-fi cult classic 'The Last Starfighter' finally scores a sequel, but it's a comic book! (exclusive)
summary: The 1980s sci-fi cult classic 'The Last Starfighter' is getting a sequel in the form of a comic book. The new project aims to continue the adventure for a new generation of fans.

title: What's the best time to see Lyrid meteors in 2026?
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower is expected to peak in 2026, potentially offering a spectacular display of fireballs and shooting stars. The best time to view the event is approaching.

title: Only 12 people on Earth saw this 'ring-of-fire' eclipse. Here's how one improvised to capture a once-in-a-lifetime photo from Antarctica
summary: A remote Antarctic research team witnessed a rare annular solar eclipse, the only people on Earth to do so. A scientist improvised to capture the event due to limited resources.

title: Watch SpaceX launch advanced GPS satellite for US Space Force early on April 21
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch an advanced GPS satellite for the U.S. Space Force on April 21, with the event available to watch live.

title: Giant BlueBird 7 mobile phone satellite will be deorbited after faulty Blue Origin launch
summary: Blue Origin successfully recovered its first reused orbital-class rocket, but the payload, the Giant BlueBird 7 mobile phone satellite, was left in the wrong orbit and will be deorbited.

title: The Lyrid meteor shower returns with ideal viewing conditions! Here's what to look out for this week
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower is returning with ideal viewing conditions, featuring fast and bright meteors under dark, moonless skies before dawn.

title: Telescope livestream: Watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades on April 19
summary: A telescope livestream will allow viewers to watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades star cluster on April 19. This celestial event can be observed from home via the livestream.

title: Synthetic universe allows you to 'see and hear' galaxies evolving from the dawn of time (video)
summary: Scientists created a synthetic universe simulation that accurately models galaxy evolution from the dawn of time, fooling some astronomers into thinking it's real.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. This adds to the growing evidence of commercial data's role in scientific applications.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA program will host a webinar on April 29 featuring MDA Space as a vendor. The event aims to provide insights into the CSDA program.

title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
summary: NASA's Human Research Program (HRP) is developing methods to safeguard astronaut health for long-term missions to the Moon and Mars using ground research, the ISS, and analog environments. The Artemis II mission was the first step in this effort.

title: NASA, OPM Announce New NASA Force Website, Open Job Applications
summary: NASA and the U.S. Office of Personnel Management launched the NASA Force website to recruit engineers and technologists for mission-critical roles in NASA's programs. The initiative aims to support America's air and space missions through high-impact technical talent hiring.

title: Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Tomorrow.io Precipitation Radar Data
summary: A quality assessment report has evaluated the precipitation radar data from Tomorrow.io, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. This adds to the growing evidence of commercial data's value in scientific applications.

title: Volunteers Discover Rare Space Weather Events Using Their Ears
summary: Volunteers are helping scientists analyze rare space weather events by listening to data sonified from Earth's magnetic field, comparing it to a 'giant harp in space.' The NASA HARP citizen science project transforms magnetic field data into audible sounds for analysis.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Observatory Maps Interstellar Ice in Milky Way
summary: NASA's SPHEREx Observatory has mapped interstellar ice, including water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, in the Cygnus X region of the Milky Way. This discovery highlights the chemical complexity of star-forming regions in our galaxy.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords in a ceremony hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman at NASA Headquarters. The event will include Latvia's minister for education and science and other officials.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side near the terminator, highlighting long shadows and the Orientale Basin. The image provides a detailed view of lunar surface features under low-angle sunlight.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the New Glenn launch. The incident follows Blue Origin's recent milestone of landing a flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a new Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This marks a significant step in preparing for commercial space station operations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests, marking a significant milestone for the Artemis program following the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew members conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures on the International Space Station in March. The month was marked by significant activities onboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will conduct its third launch using a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo ship to the International Space Station (ISS). This mission follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission, which concluded the CRS NG-23 flight.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details about the news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement has been highlighted as optimal.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at ESA's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in pointing mechanisms for spacecraft, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency in directional control systems. It highlights innovations in technology that enhance spacecraft maneuverability.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team conducted experiments with graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, demonstrating light-based propulsion in microgravity. The findings suggest potential applications for steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions using lasers.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material for future space missions.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology. This innovation could impact various sectors within the space industry.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking another international partner in the program. Mike Gold of Redwire emphasized the need for countries to move beyond signing to actively contributing to Artemis.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX secured a $175.7M contract to launch ESA's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars by late 2028, but NASA's potential withdrawal due to budget cuts could jeopardize the mission.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is entering the thruster market with its new Gauss product line, aiming to vertically integrate and address scalability concerns for customers. The move is part of Rocket Lab's strategy to expand its offerings beyond launch services.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing its first qualification spacesuit for a 2027 flight, with production ongoing despite external developments. The company's chief astronaut confirmed the suit's progress in an interview with Payload.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026. Axiom Space was previously the sole provider for NASA's Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs), but new competitors are now emerging.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key industry challenges such as sustainability, regulatory issues, national security, and international collaboration. The council aims to shape strategic decisions for the space sector.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: The ORBX ETF has been launched to track companies earning over half their revenue from space technology, including transportation, software, and satellite data services. This launch coincides with a wave of space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The announcement was made by the companies involved.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion of the space community while questioning their financial planning expertise.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's claims, to which NASA responds by asking for evidence. The dispute was discussed at the Space Symposium.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted announcements from satellite companies promoting in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Via Satellite provided a roundup of key developments in this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships. The article highlights this trend in the aviation industry.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based sensors for tracking objects in orbit.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has chosen Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the third phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology for space.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space threats in a recent discussion.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI). Officials, including Meink, stated that the project carries low risk.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. The platform aims to provide improved performance for space missions.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The platform integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Reductions in satellite launch prices and growing demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers are driving interest in orbital space data centers. The industry is exploring various architectural options for these orbital facilities.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's (SDA) Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's new acquisition structure rollout.
